summary: Brand hierarchy and placement rules for all Sellsuki ecosystem products

level_1.name: Ecosystem Brand
level_1.role: top-of-page ecosystem signature shared across all products

level_2.name: Product Brand
level_2.role: product identity — secondary to ecosystem, clearly differentiated but not competing
level_2.note: Product identity can be shown but must be visually secondary to Sellsuki

required: Sellsuki is the ecosystem brand — placed in topnav
Topnav is always the ecosystem brand layer
Sidebar is always the product navigation layer
Product identity is secondary layer — cannot override ecosystem
forbidden: Primary brand placed in sidebar as default
Topnav used as page title bar only (stripped of brand meaning)
Each product having different shell brand signature without governance
Product brand visually competing with Sellsuki brand

ai_behavior.default_when_unspecified: Sellsuki is ecosystem brand in topnav; product identity is secondary in sidebar
must_do: Treat product brand as secondary to Sellsuki
Use semantic color tokens — Sky for primary, Gray for structure
Use DB HeaventRounded for all text elements
must_not_do: Place primary ecosystem logo in sidebar as default
Give each product a unique shell brand signature without governance
Hardcode brand colors — always use design tokens
ai_behavior.escalate_when: Existing product uses sidebar-led brand — flag as legacy, do not copy

compliance_checklist: Sellsuki ecosystem brand is visible in topnav
Sidebar does not hold primary Sellsuki logo
Product brand identity is secondary and clearly differentiated
DB HeaventRounded is used for all text
Semantic color tokens used — no hardcoded hex
Light theme by default unless overridden
Brand hierarchy is clear at first glance
Shell structure is consistent with canonical AppShell 2.0

mcp_resources.description: Read brand hierarchy and placement contract
Design token requirements for brand compliance
